title: Nick's Perfect Smashburger
description: The crispiest, juiciest smashburger you'll ever make. Two thin patties smashed hard on a screaming-hot skillet with melted American cheese.

steps.title: Prep the meat
steps.body: Divide ground beef into 4 equal balls, about 100g each. Don't season yet.

steps.title: Heat the skillet
steps.body: Get a cast iron skillet screaming hot over high heat for 3 minutes. You want it smoking.

steps.title: Smash the patties
steps.body: Place a ball on the skillet, cover with parchment, and press flat with a heavy spatula. Season with salt and pepper. Cook for 2 minutes without moving.

steps.title: Flip and cheese
steps.body: Flip each patty, immediately lay a cheese slice on top. Cook 1 more minute until cheese melts.

steps.title: Toast the buns
steps.body: Butter the cut side of each bun and toast face-down in the skillet for 30 seconds until golden.

steps.title: Stack and serve
steps.body: Stack two patties per bun. Add pickles, thinly sliced onion. Eat immediately — these don't wait.

notes: The key is not touching the patties once they're smashed. Let the crust form. 80/20 beef is non-negotiable — leaner meat makes a dry burger.

title: Garlic Butter Steak
description: Reverse-seared ribeye finished with garlic butter baste. Restaurant-quality steak at home with a perfect medium-rare center.

steps.title: Temper the steak
steps.body: Take steaks out of the fridge 30 minutes before cooking. Pat dry with paper towels and season generously with salt and pepper on all sides.

steps.title: Oven low and slow
steps.body: Preheat oven to 120°C (250°F). Place steaks on a wire rack over a sheet pan. Cook until internal temp hits 50°C (122°F), about 20 minutes.

steps.title: Sear hard
steps.body: Heat a cast iron skillet over high heat with oil until smoking. Sear steaks 90 seconds per side for a deep brown crust.

steps.title: Butter baste
steps.body: Add butter, crushed garlic, thyme, and rosemary to the pan. Tilt and spoon the foaming butter over steaks for 1 minute.

steps.title: Rest and slice
steps.body: Rest steaks on a cutting board for 5 minutes. Slice against the grain, pour pan butter over the top.

notes: Reverse sear gives you edge-to-edge pink with a hard crust. Use a meat thermometer — guessing wastes good steak.

title: Salmon Teriyaki Bowl
description: Crispy-skin salmon over sushi rice with homemade teriyaki glaze, avocado, and quick-pickled cucumber. Clean, balanced, beautiful.

steps.title: Cook the rice
steps.body: Rinse sushi rice until water runs clear. Cook with 1.75 cups water. Once done, fold in rice vinegar.

steps.title: Make teriyaki glaze
steps.body: Combine soy sauce, mirin, and honey in a small saucepan. Simmer over medium heat for 3 minutes until slightly thickened.

steps.title: Quick-pickle cucumber
steps.body: Thinly slice cucumber. Toss with a splash of rice vinegar and a pinch of salt. Set aside.

steps.title: Sear the salmon
steps.body: Pat salmon dry. Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high. Place skin-side down and cook 4 minutes until skin is crispy. Flip and cook 2 more minutes.

steps.title: Assemble bowls
steps.body: Divide rice between bowls. Top with salmon, sliced avocado, pickled cucumber. Drizzle teriyaki glaze and sprinkle sesame seeds.

notes: Start skin-side down in a cold-ish pan and don't move it. The skin will release when it's crispy. If it sticks, it's not ready.

title: Creamy Pasta Carbonara
description: Real carbonara — no cream. Just eggs, pecorino, guanciale, and black pepper. Silky, rich, and done in 20 minutes.

steps.title: Boil pasta
steps.body: Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to boil. Cook spaghetti 1 minute short of al dente. Save 1 cup pasta water before draining.

steps.title: Render guanciale
steps.body: Cut guanciale into small strips. Cook in a cold skillet over medium heat for 5 minutes until crispy and fat renders out. Remove from heat.

steps.title: Make the egg mixture
steps.body: Whisk yolks, whole egg, finely grated pecorino, and a generous amount of cracked black pepper in a bowl.

steps.title: Combine off heat
steps.body: Add drained pasta to the guanciale pan OFF the heat. Toss. Pour in egg mixture, toss vigorously. Add pasta water a splash at a time until creamy and glossy.

steps.title: Serve immediately
steps.body: Plate with extra pecorino and black pepper on top. Eat right away — carbonara waits for no one.

notes: The pan must be OFF the heat when you add the eggs. Hot pan = scrambled eggs, not carbonara. Toss fast, add pasta water until it looks like a sauce.

title: World's Best Grilled Cheese
description: Sourdough, three cheeses, garlic butter on the outside. Crunchy, gooey, and better than any restaurant version.

steps.title: Make garlic butter
steps.body: Melt butter in a small bowl. Grate garlic into it and mix well.

steps.title: Build the sandwich
steps.body: Spread a thin layer of dijon on the inside of each bread slice. Layer gruyère, cheddar, and mozzarella between slices.

steps.title: Butter the outside
steps.body: Brush garlic butter generously on the outer sides of the bread.

steps.title: Cook low and slow
steps.body: Place in a skillet over medium-low heat. Cover with a lid. Cook 3 minutes per side until golden brown and cheese is melted.

steps.title: Slice and serve
steps.body: Cut diagonally. Serve immediately while the cheese pull is at its peak.

notes: Medium-low heat is the secret. High heat browns the bread before the cheese melts. The lid traps steam and melts the cheese faster.

title: Crispy Chicken Thighs
description: Bone-in chicken thighs with impossibly crispy skin. Started in a cold pan for maximum crunch, finished in the oven.

steps.title: Season the chicken
steps.body: Pat thighs very dry with paper towels. Season both sides with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and smoked paprika.

steps.title: Cold pan start
steps.body: Place thighs skin-side down in a cold cast iron skillet with oil. Turn heat to medium. Cook without moving for 12 minutes as the fat renders and skin crisps.

steps.title: Flip and oven
steps.body: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Flip thighs. Transfer skillet to oven for 15 minutes until internal temp hits 74°C (165°F).

steps.title: Rest and serve
steps.body: Rest 5 minutes. Squeeze lemon over top. The skin should shatter when you bite into it.

notes: Starting in a cold pan is the trick. The skin renders its fat slowly, creating a cracker-like crust. Don't skip drying the skin.

title: Chocolate Chip Cookies
description: Browned butter, two types of chocolate, flaky salt on top. Chewy center, crispy edges. Better than bakery.

steps.title: Brown the butter
steps.body: Melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Swirl until it turns golden brown and smells nutty, about 4 minutes. Pour into a large bowl and let cool 5 minutes.

steps.title: Mix wet ingredients
steps.body: Add both sugars to the browned butter and whisk until smooth. Add egg, yolk, and vanilla. Whisk until thick and glossy.

steps.title: Add dry and chocolate
steps.body: Fold in flour, baking soda, and salt until just combined. Fold in both chocolate chips. Don't overmix.

steps.title: Scoop and bake
steps.body: Preheat oven to 190°C (375°F). Scoop balls onto a lined baking sheet, spaced 5cm apart. Bake 10 minutes until edges are set but centers look underdone.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sprinkle flaky salt on top immediately. Let cool on the sheet for 5 minutes — they firm up as they cool. The center should be gooey.

notes: Browning the butter is what makes these special. Don't overbake — pull them when they look slightly underdone. They set as they cool.

title: Miso Salmon
description: White miso-glazed salmon caramelized under the broiler. Sweet, savory, and impossibly easy for how good it tastes.

steps.title: Make the glaze
steps.body: Mix miso, mirin, soy sauce, honey, and sesame oil in a bowl until smooth.

steps.title: Marinate
steps.body: Coat salmon with the glaze. If you have time, marinate 30 minutes. If not, move to the next step — it's still great.

steps.title: Broil
steps.body: Set oven to broil. Place salmon on a foil-lined sheet pan. Broil 6 minutes for medium, 8 for well done. The glaze should caramelize and char slightly.

steps.title: Garnish and serve
steps.body: Top with sliced green onions. Serve with rice and steamed vegetables.

notes: Watch it under the broiler — miso burns fast. The perfect spot is deep golden with tiny charred spots. 6 minutes is usually right.

title: Spicy Dan Dan Noodles
description: Sichuan-style noodles with chili oil, ground pork, and a nutty sesame sauce. Bold, spicy, and deeply savory.

steps.title: Make the sauce
steps.body: Mix tahini, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and 2 tbsp chili oil in a bowl. Add a splash of hot water to loosen.

steps.title: Cook pork
steps.body: Brown ground pork in a wok over high heat with minced garlic and ginger for 4 minutes. Break it up as it cooks.

steps.title: Toast Sichuan peppercorns
steps.body: Push pork to the side, toast peppercorns in the empty part of the wok for 30 seconds until fragrant.

steps.title: Boil noodles
steps.body: Cook noodles in boiling water according to package. Drain, saving 1/4 cup cooking water.

steps.title: Combine and serve
steps.body: Divide sauce between bowls. Add noodles, pork, remaining chili oil, chopped green onions, and crushed peanuts. Mix at the table.

notes: Sichuan peppercorns create a numbing sensation — that's supposed to happen. Adjust chili oil to your heat tolerance.

title: Classic French Omelette
description: Silky, custardy, no color. The technique test every chef respects. Three eggs, butter, 90 seconds.

steps.title: Beat the eggs
steps.body: Crack 3 eggs into a bowl. Add salt. Beat with a fork until completely uniform — no white streaks.

steps.title: Cook fast
steps.body: Melt butter in a non-stick pan over medium-high heat. When foaming subsides, pour in eggs. Stir constantly with a chopstick or fork while shaking the pan for 45 seconds.

steps.title: Shape and roll
steps.body: When eggs are just set but still custardy on top, stop stirring. Tilt pan and roll the omelette onto itself. Slide onto a plate seam-side down.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Top with a tiny knob of butter, crème fraîche, and snipped chives. Should be pale yellow — no browning.

notes: This is a speed test. Total pan time should be under 90 seconds. If the eggs brown, the pan is too hot. Practice makes perfect.

title: Chicken Tikka Masala
description: Yogurt-marinated chicken in a rich, spiced tomato-cream sauce. The comfort dish that bridges two continents.

steps.title: Marinate chicken
steps.body: Cut chicken into 3cm cubes. Toss with yogurt, 1 tsp garam masala, turmeric, and salt. Marinate at least 20 minutes.

steps.title: Char the chicken
steps.body: Heat a skillet over high heat. Sear marinated chicken pieces for 3 minutes per side until charred. Don't cook through — they'll finish in the sauce.

steps.title: Build the sauce
steps.body: In the same pan, melt butter. Sauté diced onion for 5 minutes. Add minced garlic and ginger, cook 1 minute. Add remaining spices, stir 30 seconds.

steps.title: Simmer
steps.body: Pour in crushed tomatoes. Simmer 10 minutes. Stir in cream. Add chicken back. Simmer 10 more minutes until chicken is cooked through.

steps.title: Serve
steps.body: Garnish with cilantro. Serve over basmati rice or with warm naan.

notes: Charring the chicken before simmering is what separates good tikka masala from great. The yogurt marinade keeps it tender.

title: Avocado Toast with a Twist
description: Sourdough, smashed avocado, a fried egg, chili flakes, and everything bagel seasoning. Elevated breakfast in 5 minutes.

steps.title: Toast the bread
steps.body: Toast sourdough slices until golden and crunchy.

steps.title: Smash the avocado
steps.body: Halve the avocado, scoop into a bowl. Add lemon juice, salt, and mash with a fork. Leave it chunky.

steps.title: Fry the egg
steps.body: Heat olive oil in a small pan over medium heat. Crack egg in, cook until whites set but yolk is still runny, about 2 minutes.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.body: Spread avocado on toast. Top with fried egg. Sprinkle red pepper flakes, everything bagel seasoning, and flaky salt.

notes: The egg yolk is the sauce. When you cut into it, it should run over the avocado. Undercook the yolk if anything.

title: Thai Basil Chicken
description: Pad krapao — Thailand's fast-food staple. Wok-fried chicken with holy basil, chili, garlic, over jasmine rice with a fried egg.

steps.title: Prep aromatics
steps.body: Roughly chop garlic and chilies together — you want them bruised, not minced. Keep basil leaves whole.

steps.title: Wok the chicken
steps.body: Heat oil in a wok over high heat until smoking. Add garlic and chilies, stir 15 seconds. Add chicken, break up, cook 4 minutes until browned.

steps.title: Season
steps.body: Add soy sauce, oyster sauce, fish sauce, and sugar. Toss for 1 minute. Kill the heat, toss in basil leaves. Stir until wilted.

steps.title: Fry eggs
steps.body: In a separate pan, fry eggs in oil over high heat until edges are crispy and lacy. Yolk stays runny.

steps.title: Plate
steps.body: Serve chicken over jasmine rice, top with fried egg. Mix everything together when eating.

notes: Holy basil (krapao) is different from sweet basil — it has a peppery, clove-like flavor. If you can't find it, sweet basil works but the dish changes.

title: Birria Tacos
description: Slow-braised, chili-stewed beef tucked into crispy tortillas and dipped in consommé. The taco that broke the internet.

steps.title: Toast chiles
steps.body: Remove stems and seeds from dried chiles. Toast in a dry pan for 1 minute per side until fragrant. Cover with hot water and soak 20 minutes.

steps.title: Blend the sauce
steps.body: Blend soaked chiles with onion, garlic, cumin, oregano, cloves, vinegar, and 1 cup soaking liquid until smooth.

steps.title: Braise the beef
steps.body: Cut beef into large chunks. Sear in a Dutch oven for 3 minutes per side. Pour chile sauce and broth over. Cover and braise at 160°C (325°F) for 2.5 hours until falling apart.

steps.title: Shred and prep
steps.body: Remove beef, shred with forks. Strain the braising liquid — that's your consommé. Skim fat from consommé into a small bowl.

steps.title: Make the tacos
steps.body: Dip tortillas in the consommé fat. Griddle until pliable, add shredded cheese and beef. Fold and cook 2 minutes per side until crispy.

steps.title: Serve
steps.body: Serve tacos with a bowl of hot consommé for dipping. Top with cilantro and diced onion.

notes: The consommé is everything — never skip it. The tacos get dipped into it like a French dip. Make extra; it freezes well.

title: Garlic Fried Rice
description: Day-old rice stir-fried with an outrageous amount of garlic, soy sauce, and a crispy fried egg. 10-minute leftover magic.

steps.title: Crisp the garlic
steps.body: Thinly slice garlic. Fry in 2 tbsp oil over medium heat for 2 minutes until golden. Remove chips, keep the oil.

steps.title: Fry the rice
steps.body: Crank heat to high. Add rice to the garlic oil. Spread flat and let it sear for 2 minutes without stirring. Then toss.

steps.title: Season
steps.body: Add soy sauce, sesame oil, and white pepper. Toss for 1 minute. Push rice to the side, scramble eggs in the gap, then fold together.

steps.title: Serve
steps.body: Top with garlic chips and sliced green onions. Fry an extra egg on top if you want to be a legend.

notes: Day-old rice is mandatory — fresh rice is too wet and steams instead of frying. If you're in a rush, spread fresh rice on a sheet pan in the fridge for 30 minutes.

title: Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in a spiced tomato sauce with cumin, paprika, and feta. One pan, 20 minutes, weekend breakfast perfection.

steps.title: Sauté vegetables
steps.body: Heat olive oil in a deep skillet. Dice onion and pepper. Sauté for 5 minutes until soft. Add garlic, cook 1 minute.

steps.title: Build the sauce
steps.body: Add cumin, paprika, and chili flakes. Stir 30 seconds. Pour in crushed tomatoes, season with salt. Simmer 8 minutes until slightly thickened.

steps.title: Poach the eggs
steps.body: Make 4 wells in the sauce with a spoon. Crack an egg into each well. Cover and cook 5 minutes until whites are set but yolks are runny.

steps.title: Finish and serve
steps.body: Crumble feta over top, sprinkle parsley. Serve in the skillet with crusty bread for dipping.

notes: Don't touch the eggs once they're in. Cover the pan — the steam cooks the tops. Runny yolks are the whole point.

title: Crispy Pork Belly
description: Slow-roasted pork belly with crackling skin so loud you can hear it across the room. Worth the wait.

steps.title: Score and season
steps.body: Score the skin in a crosshatch pattern without cutting into the meat. Rub the meat side with five spice and white pepper. Rub the skin side with vinegar and baking soda.

steps.title: Dry the skin
steps.body: Place on a rack in the fridge uncovered for at least 4 hours, ideally overnight. The drier the skin, the better the crackling.

steps.title: Salt crust
steps.body: Pack a thick layer of salt on the skin (it will be removed later). Wrap the sides of the pork belly in foil, leaving only the salt-crusted skin exposed.

steps.title: Slow roast
steps.body: Roast at 160°C (325°F) for 1.5 hours. The meat cooks low and slow while the skin dehydrates under the salt.

steps.title: Blast for crackling
steps.body: Remove salt crust and foil. Crank oven to 250°C (480°F). Roast 20 more minutes until the skin puffs and crackles.

steps.title: Rest and slice
steps.body: Rest 10 minutes. Slice into thick strips. The skin should shatter.

notes: Patience is the only hard part. The salt crust draws moisture out of the skin, which is what creates the crackling. Don't skip the fridge drying step.

title: Lemon Herb Pasta
description: Bright, lemony pasta with garlic, parmesan, and a handful of fresh herbs. Dead simple, impossibly fresh.

steps.title: Cook pasta
steps.body: Boil pasta in salted water until al dente. Save 1 cup pasta water.

steps.title: Garlic oil
steps.body: While pasta cooks, heat olive oil over medium. Slice garlic thin and cook 2 minutes until golden. Add red pepper flakes.

steps.title: Combine
steps.body: Toss drained pasta into the garlic oil. Add lemon zest, lemon juice, and half the parmesan. Add pasta water until saucy.

steps.title: Herbs and serve
steps.body: Kill heat, toss in torn basil and parsley. Top with remaining parmesan.

notes: Add the lemon off heat to prevent bitterness. The herbs go in last — heat kills their flavor. This pasta is about freshness.

title: Korean Beef Bowl
description: Bulgogi-inspired ground beef over rice with gochujang, pickled veg, and a runny egg. 15 minutes, one pan, weeknight legend.

steps.title: Make sauce
steps.body: Whisk soy sauce, gochujang, brown sugar, sesame oil, minced garlic, and ginger.

steps.title: Cook beef
steps.body: Brown ground beef over high heat for 4 minutes. Drain excess fat. Pour sauce over and toss for 1 minute.

steps.title: Quick pickle
steps.body: Thinly slice cucumber, toss with rice vinegar and a pinch of salt.

steps.title: Fry eggs
steps.body: Fry eggs sunny-side up in a bit of oil. Runny yolks mandatory.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.body: Rice in bowl, beef on top, pickled cucumber on the side, fried egg on top. Sprinkle sesame seeds and green onions.

notes: Gochujang is fermented chili paste — sweet, spicy, and funky. Find it in the Asian aisle. It keeps forever in the fridge.

title: Butter Chicken
description: Tandoori-spiced chicken in a velvety tomato-butter sauce. The dish that made Indian food global. Rich, mild, crowd-pleasing.

steps.title: Marinate chicken
steps.body: Cut chicken into cubes. Marinate in yogurt, half the garam masala, chili powder, and salt for 20 minutes minimum.

steps.title: Cook chicken
steps.body: Melt 2 tbsp butter over high heat. Sear marinated chicken 3 minutes per side. Set aside.

steps.title: Make the sauce
steps.body: In the same pan, add remaining butter. Sauté diced onion 5 minutes. Add garlic and ginger, cook 1 minute. Add tomato puree and remaining spices. Simmer 10 minutes.

steps.title: Blend and finish
steps.body: Blend sauce until smooth (immersion blender or regular). Return to pan. Add cream, sugar, fenugreek. Simmer 5 minutes. Add chicken back.

steps.title: Serve
steps.body: Serve over basmati rice. Drizzle with a spoon of cream on top.

notes: Kashmiri chili is mild and gives color without heat. If using regular chili powder, halve the amount. The sugar balances the tomato acidity.

title: Mushroom Risotto
description: Creamy arborio rice with sautéed mushrooms, white wine, and parmesan. Stirring meditation that pays off with every spoonful.

steps.title: Sear mushrooms
steps.body: Heat olive oil over high heat. Sear mushrooms without moving for 3 minutes until browned. Flip, cook 2 more minutes. Season, remove.

steps.title: Start the risotto
steps.body: In the same pan, melt 1 tbsp butter. Sauté finely diced onion for 3 minutes. Add garlic, stir 30 seconds. Add rice, toast for 2 minutes until edges turn translucent.

steps.title: Deglaze
steps.body: Pour in white wine. Stir until fully absorbed, about 1 minute.

steps.title: Ladle and stir
steps.body: Add warm broth one ladle at a time, stirring between each addition until absorbed. Repeat for 18 minutes until rice is al dente and creamy.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Remove from heat. Stir in remaining butter, parmesan, thyme leaves, and mushrooms. Let rest 2 minutes — it should flow like lava when plated.

notes: Never rinse risotto rice — you need the surface starch. Keep the broth warm in a separate pot. Cold broth stops the cooking and makes it gummy.

title: Fish Tacos
description: Beer-battered white fish in warm tortillas with lime crema, shredded cabbage, and pickled onion. Beach food that hits anywhere.

steps.title: Pickle onion
steps.body: Thinly slice red onion. Toss with rice vinegar and a pinch of salt. Set aside — 15 minutes minimum.

steps.title: Make lime crema
steps.body: Mix sour cream with juice of 1 lime and a pinch of salt.

steps.title: Make batter
steps.body: Whisk flour, baking powder, salt, and beer until smooth. Should be like thin pancake batter.

steps.title: Fry the fish
steps.body: Heat oil to 190°C (375°F). Cut fish into strips. Dip in batter, letting excess drip off. Fry 3 minutes until golden and crispy.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.body: Warm tortillas on a dry skillet. Shredded cabbage, fried fish, lime crema, pickled onion, squeeze of lime. Eat with your hands.

notes: Use a light beer — it keeps the batter crispy. The oil temperature matters: too cool and the batter absorbs oil, too hot and it burns before the fish cooks.

title: Homemade Ramen
description: Rich shoyu broth, soft-boiled eggs, chashu pork, and fresh noodles. A weekend project that's better than takeout.

steps.title: Make chashu
steps.body: Roll pork belly and tie with string. Sear all sides in a hot pan. Simmer in soy sauce (2 tbsp), mirin, and 1 cup water for 45 minutes. Slice when cool.

steps.title: Soft-boil eggs
steps.body: Boil eggs for exactly 6.5 minutes. Ice bath immediately. Peel and marinate in leftover chashu liquid.

steps.title: Build the broth
steps.body: Simmer chicken broth with smashed garlic, sliced ginger, and remaining soy sauce for 20 minutes. Strain. Add sesame oil.

steps.title: Cook noodles
steps.body: Boil noodles according to package — slightly underdone. Drain well.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.body: Noodles in bowl. Hot broth poured over. Top with sliced chashu, halved egg, green onions, and nori sheet.

notes: The chashu braising liquid is liquid gold — use it to marinate the eggs and add a splash to the final broth. Everything connects.

title: Caprese Salad
description: Fresh mozzarella, peak-season tomatoes, basil, olive oil, and flaky salt. Nothing to hide behind — ingredients are everything.

steps.title: Slice
steps.body: Slice tomatoes and mozzarella into 1cm-thick rounds.

steps.title: Arrange
steps.body: Alternate tomato and mozzarella on a plate, slightly overlapping. Tuck basil leaves between slices.

steps.title: Dress
steps.body: Drizzle generously with olive oil and optional balsamic glaze. Finish with flaky salt and cracked pepper.

notes: This dish lives or dies on tomato quality. If tomatoes aren't in season, don't make this. Use burrata instead of mozzarella to go next level.

title: Honey Garlic Shrimp
description: Seared shrimp in a sticky honey-garlic-soy glaze. Dinner in 10 minutes that looks like you tried way harder than you did.

steps.title: Sear shrimp
steps.body: Pat shrimp dry. Sear in a hot pan with 1 tbsp butter for 1 minute per side until pink. Remove.

steps.title: Make the glaze
steps.body: In the same pan, add remaining butter, minced garlic, cook 30 seconds. Add honey, soy sauce, lemon juice, and pepper flakes. Simmer 1 minute until sticky.

steps.title: Toss and serve
steps.body: Return shrimp, toss to coat. Serve immediately over rice or with bread to soak up the sauce.

notes: Dry shrimp = good sear. Wet shrimp = steamed shrimp. Paper towels are your friend. Don't overcook — shrimp go from perfect to rubbery in 30 seconds.

title: Banana Pancakes
description: Fluffy pancakes with caramelized banana, maple syrup, and a dusting of powdered sugar. Saturday morning perfection.

steps.title: Make batter
steps.body: Mix flour, baking powder, and sugar. In another bowl, whisk milk, egg, vanilla, and 1 tbsp melted butter. Combine wet into dry — stir until just mixed. Lumps are fine.

steps.title: Caramelize banana
steps.body: Slice 1 banana. Melt 1 tbsp butter, cook banana slices 1 minute per side until golden. Set aside.

steps.title: Cook pancakes
steps.body: Melt butter on a griddle or pan over medium heat. Pour 1/4 cup batter per pancake. Cook 2 minutes until bubbles form on surface, flip, cook 1 more minute.

steps.title: Stack and serve
steps.body: Stack pancakes, top with caramelized banana, sliced fresh banana, maple syrup, and powdered sugar.

notes: Don't overmix the batter — overmixed = tough pancakes. The lumps cook out. Medium heat is key; too hot and the outside burns before the inside cooks.

title: Cacio e Pepe
description: Three ingredients: pasta, pecorino, black pepper. The simplest pasta in Rome and one of the hardest to nail. Creamy, sharp, peppery.

steps.title: Toast pepper
steps.body: Toast whole black peppercorns in a dry pan for 2 minutes until fragrant. Crack coarsely with mortar and pestle or pan bottom.

steps.title: Cook pasta
steps.body: Boil pasta in less water than usual (concentrated starch). Cook to al dente. Save 2 cups pasta water.

steps.title: Make the cream
steps.body: Finely grate pecorino into a bowl. Add 1/2 cup warm pasta water and whisk into a thick paste.

steps.title: Combine
steps.body: Add pasta to the pepper pan off heat. Add pecorino paste. Toss vigorously, adding pasta water bit by bit until creamy and emulsified. The cheese should coat every strand.

steps.title: Serve
steps.body: Plate immediately with extra pecorino and pepper on top. Eat fast — it tightens as it cools.

notes: The enemy is clumps. Pan must be off heat when adding cheese. Warm pasta water emulsifies the pecorino. Too hot = cheese seizes. Too cold = doesn't melt. It's a feel thing.

title: Green Smoothie Bowl
description: Frozen banana, spinach, mango, and coconut milk blended thick. Topped with granola, coconut flakes, and fresh fruit. Breakfast that feels like dessert.

steps.title: Blend
steps.body: Blend frozen banana, mango, spinach, and coconut milk until thick and smooth. Add honey if needed. Should be thicker than a smoothie — spoonable.

steps.title: Top and serve
steps.body: Pour into a bowl. Top with granola, coconut flakes, chia seeds, and any fresh fruit you have. Eat with a spoon.

notes: Frozen fruit is the key to thickness. Fresh fruit makes it watery. Freeze bananas the night before — peel them first or you'll regret it.

title: Bruschetta
description: Grilled bread rubbed with garlic, topped with diced tomatoes, basil, and olive oil. The Italian appetizer that never gets old.

steps.title: Make topping
steps.body: Dice tomatoes, remove seeds. Toss with torn basil, 2 tbsp olive oil, balsamic, and salt. Let sit 5 minutes.

steps.title: Grill bread
steps.body: Slice bread 2cm thick. Brush with olive oil. Grill or broil 1 minute per side until charred edges.

steps.title: Rub and top
steps.body: Rub a raw garlic clove across the hot bread surface. Spoon tomato mixture on top. Extra drizzle of olive oil.

notes: Hot bread + raw garlic rub = the flavor base. It's like a garlic bread without being garlic bread. Make it right before serving — soggy bruschetta is a crime.

title: Chicken Caesar Salad
description: Grilled chicken, romaine, homemade Caesar dressing with anchovy and parmesan, garlic croutons. The salad that's actually a meal.

steps.title: Make dressing
steps.body: Mince anchovies and garlic into a paste. Whisk with egg yolk, mustard, and lemon juice. Slowly drizzle in olive oil while whisking to emulsify. Stir in half the grated parmesan.

steps.title: Grill chicken
steps.body: Season chicken with salt and pepper. Grill or pan-sear 5 minutes per side until cooked through. Rest 5 minutes, then slice.

steps.title: Make croutons
steps.body: Cube bread, toss with olive oil, garlic salt. Toast in a pan over medium heat for 3 minutes, stirring often.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.body: Tear romaine into bite-size pieces. Toss with dressing. Top with sliced chicken, croutons, shaved parmesan, and cracked pepper.

notes: Real Caesar dressing uses anchovy — it doesn't taste fishy, it tastes savory. If you skip it, you're making ranch. Make the dressing thick; it clings better.

title: Pesto Chicken Sandwich
description: Grilled chicken with fresh basil pesto, mozzarella, sun-dried tomatoes, and arugula on ciabatta. Lunch that ends the debate.

steps.title: Make pesto
steps.body: Blend basil, pine nuts, parmesan, garlic, and olive oil until smooth. Season with salt.

steps.title: Grill chicken
steps.body: Pound chicken to even thickness. Season and grill 4 minutes per side. Rest, then slice.

steps.title: Toast ciabatta
steps.body: Halve rolls, brush with olive oil, toast cut-side down until golden.

steps.title: Build sandwich
steps.body: Spread pesto on both halves. Layer mozzarella, chicken, sun-dried tomatoes, and arugula. Press and serve.

notes: Pound the chicken thin for even cooking. The pesto is the star — don't skimp. Fresh basil pesto is 10x better than jarred.
